feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Event Mesh
feed.subtitle: SAP Event Mesh Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: AIF Setup for Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am working on POC of AIF setup for Event. For this I am using Topic "sap/s4/beh/businesspartner/v1/BusinessPartner/*" to trigger BusinessPartner. 

 Does anybody know the step-by-step process to setup AIF for Event.

Please note that I have referred below link of  SAP Help Portal.

Define Event Settings | SAP Help Portal

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/810dfd34f2cc4f39aa8d946b5204fd9c/d4f5487b82774e9683e1ae03b5ac0b04.html

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Filtering the Events in S4 HANA on premise System
entry.content:
Hello,

We have done the Event Enablement Setup in our system (S4 HANA on premise 2022) and we are getting all the enabled outbound topics to our Event Mesh on BTP but here we will not need every event to be coming in the event mesh queue, we need to filter the events coming to our Event Mesh.
Example: For business partner we are getting events for all the business partner created but we need event triggered only for business partner created for specific country.

Question: What are the possible ways using which we can filter the events at S4 HANA side?

entry.title: Event mesh S/4hana on-premise
entry.content:
hi,

I created a queue on the Event Mesh, how can I direct just one event to this queue? For example, when creating the Business Partner on S/4hana on-premise

I saw that it is necessary to insert the topic name of the queue but it doesn't work for me.

but it only works when I enter * but in this case it intercepts all events:

 

Does anyone know what might be missing?

thank you

entry.title: SAP CAP with Event Mesh - Error: JWT with audience is not issued for clientIds
entry.content:
we are currently trying to integrate the event mesh into our SAP CAP application. We followed this tutorial here: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/messaging/event-mesh Therefore our files looks like the following: 

event mesh config: 

{
  "options": {
    "management": true,
    "messagingrest": true,
    "messaging": true
  },
  "rules": {
    "topicRules": {
      "publishFilter": ["${namespace}/subaccounts"],
      "subscribeFilter": ["${namespace}/subaccounts"]
    },
    "queueRules": {
      "publishFilter": ["${namespace}/subaccounts"],
      "subscribeFilter": ["${namespace}/subaccounts"]
    }
  },
  "authorities": ["$ACCEPT_GRANTED_AUTHORITIES"],
  "xs-security": {
    "oauth2-configuration": {
      "credential-types": ["binding-secret", "x509"]
    }
  },
  "version": "1.1.0",
  "emname": "our-cool-event-mesh",
  "namespace": "cool/event/mesh"
}

package json: 

{
...
"cds": {
  "requires":  {
         "messaging": {
        "[production]": {
          "queue": {
            "name": "$namespace/subaccounts"
          },
          "kind": "enterprise-messaging"
        },
        "[hybrid]": {
          "kind": "enterprise-messaging-shared"
        }
      },   
}
}
}

However, when we deploy the application, we get the error: 

Error: Jwt token with audience: [ "here the event mesh client id is shown", uaa ] is not issued for these clientIds: [ here our srv application is shown ]

ERR at returnError (/home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@sap/xssec/lib/validator.js:308:25)

ERR at /home/vcap/deps/0/node_modules/@sap/xssec/lib/validator.js:392:36

 

We googled a bit about this error and we came accross this issue for the SAP cloud sdk: https://github.com/SAP/cloud-sdk-js/issues/3050 

In the ticket it says that: " it looks like the error is because the jwt is not how the xssec library expects it to be" which also the error message kind of says that a validator returned this error. However we are using the latest sap/xssec library (3.6.1) and cannot influence it any further. 

We also tried to delete the event mesh and deploy it again, but this also didnt fix the issue. 

Is this an issue with the xssec library and I need to open a ticket for SAP or do we have the event mesh falsely configured?  

Thank you very much i advance!

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh Service Key Creation failed
entry.content:
Hi All,
Last week i created an SAP Event Mesh Instance based on this tutorial, https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-enterprisemessaging-instance-create.html.

To generate this instance i used the following JSON-File based on the service descriptor that SAP offers in the Help Section.

Instance Creation JSON:

Unfortunately when creating the OAuth2.0 service Keys (for AMQP / MQTT & HTTPREST) for external connection it didnt work out. 


the JSON I used:

The System tells me that he cannot find messaging and management.

Based on the tutorial and on this blog here I choose to leave all the placeholders as delivered from the Tutorial but it still doesnt work: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/service-key-creation-for-event-mesh/qaq-p/12527257  

Can anyone Help me with this Issue?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh - Maintain source for outbound bindings
entry.content:
Hello!

First of all, I want to apologize if this question has been answered already. I am still trying to find my way around this new site, and I have not seen any reference to it.

We have copied down our Prod environment to test. I have redone all the configuration for event mesh, and this is what my outbound message looks like:

{

"type":"sap.s4.beh.product.v1.Product.Changed.v1",

"specversion":"1.0",

"source":"/default/sap.s4.beh/PROD",

"id":"0675e911-ee96-1eef-83ae-bd428725c8db",

"time":"2024-05-08T20:02:36Z",

"datacontenttype":"application/json",

"data":{"Product":"RANDOMMATERIAL"}

}

My question is, where do I go to update the source? I needs to reflect that is coming from test and not from production.

 

Thanks so much in advance!

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh - Message processing status "Retry" for failed messages
entry.content:
Hello

I have a query on the "Retry" message processing status in Cloud Integration.

When messages polled from Event Mesh using AMQP adapter fails due to some reason during the iflow processing in Cloud Integration, the messages end up in "Retry" status in the "Monitor Message Processing" view.  After the message is retried based on the configuration mentioned in Event Mesh Queue/AMQP adapter, the message should apparently go into "Failed" status but it remains in "Retry" status.

Is there an adjustment in configuration that can be done either at Event Mesh Queue or AMQP adapter, so that message processing status is displayed as "Failed" instead of "Retry" (screenshot below) ?

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh - Message processing status "Retry" for failed messages
entry.content:
Hello

I have a query on the "Retry" message processing status in Cloud Integration.

When messages polled from Event Mesh using AMQP adapter fails due to some reason during the iflow processing in Cloud Integration, the messages end up in "Retry" status in the "Monitor Message Processing" view. After the message is retried based on the configuration mentioned in Event Mesh Queue/AMQP adapter, the message should apparently go into "Failed" status but it remains in "Retry" status in Cloud Integration.

Is there an adjusment in configuration that can be done either at Event Mesh Queue or AMQP adapter in CI, so that message processing status is displayed as "Failed" instead of "Retry" in Cloud Integration?

Kindly provide your inputs. Thanks in advance.

Regards
Sudarvizhi P

entry.title: SAP CPI - Event Mesh - Message monitoring and multiple runs for iFlow
entry.content:
Hello Community!

So, I have this iFlow that consumes a message from the Event Mesh and does X. This is good most of the times, but sometimes I will see this in the log:

The process is configured to redeliver a message 1 time on the Event Mesh side, and to try reprocessing a message 1 time on the iFlow, and so when I tested this, I could see 2 runs for a failed message. 

However here I see multiple runs (3 or 4 depending on the day), and I can see all runs in status completed.  Furthermore, unlike my testing, all these runs have processed a different message from Event Mesh, so it looks like all these runs are all bundled for some reason, and I have no idea why. I have also confirmed that all runs were successful, since I see the result in the destination system.

I thought this "runs" section was showing all the runs for a given message that failed on a previous run, but this is not the case.

I know I am missing something; I just don't know what.

I am happy to provide any more details that might be needed. Any tip will be helpful!

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh - Topic Filtering - SAP S4/Hana
entry.content:
We have a SAP S4/Hana 2020 system that we are looking to utilize to publish standard business events to SAP Event Mesh. Is there the ability to publish additional properties of the business object event into the topic name hierarchy in order to be able to have more fine grained subscriptions on consumer queues in Event Mesh, or the ability to add additional properties into the event message header for filtering purposes (like business header level information related to the business object)?

For example, we have high volume scenarios where we have multiple consumer systems that will subscribe to the publishing of a particular SAP S4/Hana event via Integration Flows (e.g. PM Order, or Equipment). We want to optimize processing in such a way that consumer system APIs are only invoked for events relevant to them in order to reduce unnecessary noise (for example, different systems may wish to receive events only for particular Company Codes). Instead of doing the event filtering at an Integration Flow level or on the consumer systems themselves, it would be more optimal filtering at the event mesh / message broker level. 

Is this possible on SAP S4/Hana - Event Mesh in terms of publishing to a topic name hierarchy additional properties? It seems like Advanced Event Mesh has the ability to do wildcard filtering on topics - does standard Event Mesh also support this? Is there also the ability to add additional business data fields into the SAP S4/Hana event message using the Business Event Handling framework to reduce the amount of API call backs to retrieve the full business data (payload) for the event.

entry.title: Unable to Activate Event Mesh Webhook Subscription
entry.content:
Good day SAP community,

I created a webhook on the 4th of June, last week Tuesday, however the status remains inactive with subscription Paused. I attempted refresh, clicked resume, however, it has been on Paused.

Has anyone experienced this before and solved this? Any solution to this because I recreated the webhook and still having same issue.
I look forward to all suggestions. Thank you

entry.title: SWEC field restrictions - need to trigger event when new material is added or material quantity is
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have created a custom business object (copied BUS2032 into ZBUS2032) and have created an event (using the SAP netweaver add-on for event enablement)to be triggered to the Event Mesh whenever the sales order is changed.

We now have requirement to add restrictions - the sales order change event should be triggered when a new material is added into the sales order or if the quantity of material is changed.

How can i achieve this? I am unable to see the KWMENG field from VBAP in the change conditions fields.

Thanks,

Smohan

entry.title: SWEC filter restrictions - filter for adding new item to/deleting item from Sales order
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have done all the customization for triggering events using SAP NetWeaver event enablement Ad-on. The events are now getting triggered for Sales order creation and sales order change.

The events however need to be triggered based on certain conditions. I had two other questions regarding SWEC field restrictions for event trigger.

  1. I want the sales order change to get triggered under multiple conditions. One of them is when there is new item added or when an item id deleted from the sales order. I am unable to find a way to add this condition into SWEC.
  2. There is also a custom field in VBAK (ZZPB) which needs to be checked and the event should be triggered only when this custom field has specific values. 

So, I have put the conditions as below in SWEC

(&VBAK_ZZPB_NEW& = SO-MTS OR &VBAK_ZZPB_NEW& = SO-MTO)

AND

(&VBAK_VDATU_OLD& NE &VBAK_VDATU_NEW& OR

&VBAP_ZMENG_OLD& NE &VBAP_ZMENG_NEW& OR

&VBEP_EDATU_OLD& NE &VBEP_EDATU_NEW& OR

&VBEP_LDDAT_OLD& NE &VBEP_LDDAT_NEW& OR

&VBEP_MBDAT_OLD& NE &VBEP_MBDAT_NEW& OR

&VBEP_TDDAT_OLD& NE &VBEP_TDDAT_NEW& OR

(&VBPA_PARVW_OLD& = SH AND &VBPA_PARNR_OLD& NE &VBPA_PARNR_NEW&)

)

This condition works only if I take off the VBAK-ZZPB conditions. Once the VBAK-ZZPB condition is added, the conditional triggering ceases to work. Any ideas on how to resolve this.

 

Your inputs are highly appreciated!

Thanks,

Smohan

entry.title: SAP AEM use case : asynch outbound and inbound interface
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to build asynchronous outbound and inbound interface with our SAP Backend.
Regarding asynchronous outbound interface(SAP Backend as sender) I know we can try to use AEM to push the events to partner system(receiver) REST API. How about asynchronous inbound scenario? Can we still use AEM to pull the files/data from partner system or what should partner system support? Should they publish to AMQP queues and then later use CI to pull the messages and use AEM later to push to SAP? (or) should we avoid AEM for inbound interface alone?

For outbound asynchronous interface we have to clarify with partner system how they want.
I understood that if AEM would like to push the data then they need to develop REST API on their side to consume. If partner system would like to pull the data from AEM queues - what is the setup needed from partner system side? Should they use AMQP protocol? Currently, the connectivity options offered by partner system is MQ, CFT, ESB etc., the question is what kind of technology they need to use on their side to pull the messages from AEM Queues? Kindly let me know the points that needs to be aligned with partner system and what kind of development efforts needed from their end(like building REST API) etc., and what kind of details/information needed to be gathered from them? Thank you!

Regards,

Ramu.

entry.title: Availability of Event Mesh in SAP Integration Suite trial account
entry.content:
Hi,

As we know Event Mesh is now part of SAP Integration Suite standard license. Do we have any plan to release it in SAP Integration Suite trail account?

Regards,

Souvik

entry.title: Event Mesh (Integration Suite) service binding not possible
entry.content:
Hi all,

i am following the instructions of SAP Help for enabling the new EMIS (Event Mesh as Integration Suite capability) and would like to do the setup with S/4HANA public cloud as event sender.

I made sure that the following prerequisite is met:
https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/initiating-event-mesh

In the next step where the event message client shall be bind I am facing an Info popup which prevents me from proceeding. The step is described here (number 5):

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configure-message-client

The message says: "There are no applications in this space."

Does anyone see the cause of the issue or do I miss any step? I have attached a few screenshots.

Best regards,
Johannes

entry.title: Event not reaching Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently trying to send equipment events (Create/Change) from an S4 OP to Event Mesh. I have used various blogs as a guide and everything seems to fit. The connection test works, the daemon is running, everything is set on the BTP side and everything is green in the Channel Monitor.

However, nothing arrives in the event mesh queue.

When I look at the event queue browser, I can also find all my events there (based on the changes in the equipment)

However, I have now noticed that nothing is arriving in the Event Monitor (only the test event i made with /IWXBE/R_PRODUCE_TEST_EVENT)

Do you have any idea, what could be wrong?

Many thanks and best regards,
Sascha

entry.title: Conditional Triggering of Standard Events to Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hello all,

   There is a requirement to trigger the sales order creation event on conditional basis, not to be triggered at all the instances except when it is set to block while creating in that case only the event should trigger to event mesh. I check it in event filter config, but I could see context attributes only ID, SOURCE and TIME. 

To reflect our attributes, we need to add  '@Event.context.attribute' for the respective field of RAP entity used to define the business event type.  How can we add event context attribute to standard sales order entity?

Thank all in advance!

entry.title: Regarding dervied event of Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi all,

  As part of getting custom payload, deriving the standard event Created in the sales order by extending the standard behavior definition.. But getting the following the syntax error.. 

 

When delcaring the event name with the same name then throwing an error already exists cant define..

Please share the thoughts in resolving this issue.. 

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Advanced Event Mesh or APIM when to use what?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

The business requirement is as follows:-  when ever something changes to material (or) new material gets created then the details like quantity, price etc, to be sent to partner system.

We are in initial discussion on what to use when among Advanced Event Mesh(AEM) or APIM?

Does AEM makes sense only in case of Asynchronous publish subscribe model?

End to end message flow would be something like below.

SAP SRM -> BTP app -> Partner system.

 Can you recommend what to use when among AEM or APIM or API?

KR,

Ramu
